h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.recoheredEarlier: The 0.04% is exactly why I’m still doubtful; that jar on the bench makes the number look almost too small to carry the claim.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner would continue to demand physical proof and empirical data to overcome their skepticism about the 0.04% figure.

scored.recoheredEarlier: If the words are exact, it still seems fair
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner would continue to accept that exact words are fair while trying to separate them from unresolved framing in two columns.

scored.recoheredEarlier: The numbers make the map feel less trustworthy now
scored.statedInsightEvidence: and that is exactly the trap
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner would continue to draft a practical cart label focusing on the map's failure and the 14x size difference.
The learner will continue to refine their draft label to avoid the ecological fallacy under the tutor's guidance.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I’m still stuck on why the higher bearing is not automatically the bad joint.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: I get why the washer says don’t grab that speed yet
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner is expected to passively receive the tutor's explanation of the difference between bearings and joints.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought the person’s body gets used to the antibiotic, like the medicine stops working on the person.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner is expected to continue discussing the timing of late doses and how it affects bacterial multiplication.
